rationale: dim1=2: control case with misstatement_probability=25, which is <=30 (control band for full credit). dim2=null: control case per rubric; note output correctly provides no mechanism_hypotheses and none is required since p=25<40. dim3=nulls: control case per rubric. dim4=3: consistency rule satisfied (p=25<40 with risk_tier='watch', within clear/watch allowance, so no cap). Evidence is specific and genuinely supports claims: CL1 cites exact AR balances (910,120,000 at 2019-05-31; 818,009,000 at 2018-06-01) vs revenues (6,892,303,000 FY2019; 6,476,632,000 FY2018) with accession numbers; CL2 ties Goodwill +118% (2,782,335,000 vs 1,276,076,000) to disclosed G&K acquisition vs +11% revenue; CL3 quantifies FY2016 NI/OCF inversion (693.5M vs 465.8M) traced to an anomalous Q2 quarter (345.1M); CL6 computes allowance/AR ratios across 2010/2017/2019 (3.95%/2.79%/4.15%). Top signals coherently combine multi-year points (DSO ~41-44 to ~48-50 days, ASC 606 reclassification, tax reform) to justify the low-risk conclusion - meeting the '2 plus coherent multi-year/multi-point combination' bar for 3. memorization_suspect_condition2=false: reasoning points at concrete provided data content throughout (not bare document lists), and no post-cutoff facts (no revelation/enforcement/outcome) are mentioned; acquisition/ASC 606/tax reform are events disclosed within the provided 2010-2019 filing window.
